title: Step 1
text: Announce Code Brown and inform the Planning & Preparedness Unit through extension (3432).

title: Step 2
text: Inform the Safety & Security Manager through extension (7777).

title: Step 3
text: If there are trapped people: record names/time/location (elevator number/code); inquire number of trapped people and health status (elderly/infants/patients); provide support/instructions and reassurance until help arrives.

title: Step 4
text: Dispatch technicians to inspect, determine the cause, and prioritize releasing trapped people.

title: Step 5
text: Call the Rapid Response Team (RRT) when required.

title: Step 6
text: Call the engineer in charge to conduct follow-up inspection and detect the cause of the issue.

title: Step 7
text: If elevator maintenance is ongoing, switch off the elevator, label it as 'out of service' and under maintenance on each floor to prevent use until maintenance is completed.

title: Step 8
text: After the issue is over, report and record findings and inform the Safety & Security Manager that the system returned to operation.

title: Step 9
text: Inform DCCT that the failure/interruption ended and the system is back to work.

title: Step 10
text: If Civil Defense is involved, obtain a copy of the report including remarks and authority.
